meals.label: Meal A
meals.name: Garlic Shrimp Scampi Linguine
ings: 1 lb large shrimp, peeled and deveined
Red pepper flakes (optional, on the side for adults)
steps: Bring a large pot of salted water to boil. Cook linguine al dente per package. Reserve ¼ cup pasta water before draining.
Pat shrimp dry. Season with salt and pepper.
Heat 1 Tbsp butter and olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high. Add shrimp in a single layer — don't crowd. Cook 1–2 min per side until just pink. Remove and set aside.
Lower heat to medium. Add remaining butter and garlic. Cook 1 min until fragrant — don't let it brown.
Add white wine and lemon juice. Let simmer 2–3 min, scraping up any bits, until slightly reduced.
Add pasta and reserved pasta water. Toss to coat — the starchy water helps emulsify the sauce.
Return shrimp to the pan. Toss everything together. Add parsley, taste, adjust salt.
Serve immediately with Parmesan and red pepper flakes on the side for adults.
meals.note: 💡 The key is not overcooking the shrimp — pull them the moment they turn pink. They'll finish in the sauce.

meals.label: Meal B
meals.name: Seared Salmon with Lemon Orzo
ings: Lemon zest and fresh parsley for garnish
steps: Season salmon with salt, pepper, and garlic powder. Heat skillet over medium-high with oil. Sear salmon 2 min per side. Remove and set aside.
Melt butter in same skillet. Add garlic and shallots; cook 1–2 min. Add wine to deglaze, scraping up any bits.
Add broth; bring to simmer. Stir in orzo; cook 10 min, stirring occasionally, until tender and broth is absorbed.
Stir in cream, Parmesan, and lemon juice.
Return salmon to skillet. Add spinach; cook 2–3 min until wilted. Garnish with parsley and lemon zest.
meals.note: 💡 Stir the orzo regularly while it cooks — it sticks to the pan if you leave it alone.
